title: Antenatal follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines conditions that require additional obstetric review and preserves escalation as a clinician decision rather than an automatic workflow output.
red_flags.rationale: Supports escalation only as clinician-assessed high-risk referral documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines conditions that require additional obstetric review and preserves escalation as a clinician decision rather than an automatic workflow output.

investigations.text: Specifies gestation-linked weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal heart, fundal height, fetal movement, laboratory, ultrasound, glucose-screening, counselling, and follow-up documentation.
investigations.rationale: Directly supports mapped weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal movement, fetal heart, fundal height, laboratory, ultrasound, OGTT, counselling, and gestation-linked follow-up fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies gestation-linked weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal heart, fundal height, fetal movement, laboratory, ultrasound, glucose-screening, counselling, and follow-up documentation.

exact_section.heading: Appendix 1 — Routine Antenatal Screening and Care

referral.text: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.
referral.rationale: Directly supports routine antenatal identity, gestational context, history, risk reassessment, counselling, patient concerns, care-plan, record, follow-up, and clinician referral documentation.
referral.evidence_extract: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.

exact_section.heading: 1. Standard Scope and Purpose; 3.4 Antenatal Care; 3.4.1 Routine Antenatal Care

additions.text: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.
additions.rationale: Directly supports routine antenatal identity, gestational context, history, risk reassessment, counselling, patient concerns, care-plan, record, follow-up, and clinician referral documentation.
additions.evidence_extract: Defines routine antenatal documentation, risk review at every visit, history and record review, patient concerns, shared decisions, counselling, care plans, and high-risk referral boundaries.

exact_section.heading: 1. Standard Scope and Purpose; 3.4 Antenatal Care; 3.4.1 Routine Antenatal Care

additions.rationale: Directly supports mapped weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal movement, fetal heart, fundal height, laboratory, ultrasound, OGTT, counselling, and gestation-linked follow-up fields.
additions.evidence_extract: Specifies gestation-linked weight, blood pressure, urine dipstick, fetal heart, fundal height, fetal movement, laboratory, ultrasound, glucose-screening, counselling, and follow-up documentation.

source_limitations: The baseline includes symptom and red-flag fields not all stated in the reviewed routine sections.
High-risk and complication-specific management remains outside this mapping.
Medication or supplement decisions and fixed clinical interpretations remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
